2604.14137.title: From Feelings to Metrics: Understanding and Formalizing How Users Vibe-Test LLMs
2604.14137.paper_summary: Evaluating LLMs is challenging, as benchmark scores often fail to capture models' real-world usefulness. Instead, users often rely on ``vibe-testing'': informal experience-based evaluation, such as comparing models on coding tasks related to their own workflow. While prevalent, vibe-testing is often too ad hoc and unstructured to analyze or reproduce at scale. In this work, we study how vibe-testing works in practice and then formalize it to support systematic analysis. We first analyze two empirical resources: (1) a survey of user evaluation practices, and (2) a collection of in-the-wild model comparison reports from blogs and social media. Based on these resources, we formalize vibe-testing as a two-part process: users personalize both what they test and how they judge responses. We then introduce a proof-of-concept evaluation pipeline that follows this formulation by generating personalized prompts and comparing model outputs using user-aware subjective criteria. In experiments on coding benchmarks, we find that combining personalized prompts and user-aware evaluation can change which model is preferred, reflecting the role of vibe-testing in practice. These findings suggest that formalized vibe-testing can serve as a useful approach for bridging benchmark scores and real-world experience.
2604.14137.paper_summary_zh: 評估大型語言模型（LLMs）是具有挑戰性的，因為基準分數往往無法捕捉模型在現實世界中的實用性。相反，用戶通常依賴於「氛圍測試」：基於經驗的非正式評估，例如比較與自身工作流程相關的編碼任務模型。雖然氛圍測試很普遍，但它往往過於臨時和無結構，難以進行分析或大規模重現。在本研究中，我們研究氛圍測試在實踐中的運作方式，然後將其形式化以支持系統分析。我們首先分析兩個實證資源：（1）用戶評估實踐的調查，以及（2）來自博客和社交媒體的現場模型比較報告的集合。基於這些資源，我們將氛圍測試形式化為一個兩部分的過程：用戶個性化他們測試的內容和評判回應的方式。我們然後介紹一個概念驗證評估管道，該管道遵循這一形式化，通過生成個性化提示並使用以用戶為中心的主觀標準比較模型輸出。在編碼基準的實驗中，我們發現結合個性化提示和以用戶為中心的評估可以改變哪個模型被偏好，反映出氛圍測試在實踐中的作用。這些發現表明，形式化的氛圍測試可以作為一種有用的方法，用於橋接基準分數和現實世界經驗。
2604.14137.author: Itay Itzhak et.al.
2604.14137.authors: Itay Itzhak, Eliya Habba, Gabriel Stanovsky, Yonatan Belinkov
